title: cordyla madagascariensis subsp. tamarindoides
intro: Cordyla madagascariensis subsp. tamarindoides is a tropical plant species belonging to the family Fabaceae, commonly known as the Cordia or Bead Tree. It is a deciduous tree native to Madagascar, known for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The plant's leaves and roots have been traditionally used to treat various health issues, such as fever, diarrhea, and respiratory infections, with health benefits including the reduction of inflammation and improvement of digestive health. Herbal preparations of Cordyla madagascariensis subsp. tamarindoides include tea and infusions, made by boiling the leaves or roots in water, which are often consumed to reap its medicinal benefits.
